summary: Gruber marks Daring Fireball's 15th anniversary with a brief retrospective on its origins and output. He notes the site began in August 2002 with a piece on PowerMac G4s, and looks back at his early writing style with mild self-deprecation. The post is primarily a statistical accounting of his prolific output: over 1,174 columns and 25,485 Linked List entries totaling over 2 million original words. He summarizes the achievement with characteristic understatement: 'Not bad.'
key_points: Daring Fireball launched in August 2002 with a post about the PowerMac G4 lineup
Gruber cringes at his early affectation of referring to himself as 'the Daring Fireball' instead of using first person, but finds the writing otherwise holds up
Over 15 years, Gruber produced 1,174 full columns and 25,485 Linked List entries
The combined output totals over 2 million original words and over 3 million words including blockquotes
money_quotes: I groan at the use of 'the Daring Fireball' in lieu of the first person, but otherwise it holds up pretty well stylistically.
key_insight: Fifteen years of consistent, prolific writing on a personal blog amounts to a body of work exceeding two million original words — a testament to sustained independent publishing on the web.
